title: pilosella flagellaris subsp. subsarmentosa
intro: Pilosella flagellaris subsp. subsarmentosa is a subspecies of the Pilosella flagellaris plant, commonly known as a type of bitter plant or a relative of the dandelion. It is used in traditional medicine due to its bitter properties, which stimulate digestion and appetite. The plant is believed to have health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving liver function, and supporting the immune system. Pilosella flagellaris subsp. subsarmentosa is typically harvested for herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support digestive and liver health.
